title: Aphanes neglecta
intro: Aphanes neglecta, also known as lesser lady's mantle, is a small, annual plant in the rose family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to its reported antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Aphanes neglecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Aphanes neglecta is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in formulas to support digestive and immune system health.
taxonomy: Aphanes neglecta belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Aphanes. This plant is commonly known as a species of wood sorrel. Aphanes neglecta is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is a small annual herb with small, yellowish-green flowers.
